hook.text: New Zealand had no land mammals for 80 million years. Birds filled every ecological role.

buildup.text: Moa were the grazers. Haast's eagle was the apex predator. Kiwis foraged like small mammals.

discovery.text: Without mammals, birds evolved to fill niches that rodents, deer, and cats occupy elsewhere.

twist.text: Polynesian settlers brought rats. Europeans brought cats, stoats, and possums. Most birds crashed.

climax.text: New Zealand now runs the world's most ambitious predator-free island restoration programs.

punchline.text: A land of birds learned what mammals can destroy.

quiz.question: What filled mammal niches in New Zealand before humans arrived?
